summary: Classified the five ready-for-maintainer PRs from the hydrated preflight. No GitHub mutations are planned because close/comment/label are blocked, merge preflight is missing for all open PRs, and executable fix artifacts are blocked by either high-risk merge labels, active product-scope ambiguity, or a security-routed linked ref. Only exact hydrated security-sensitive issue #88111 is routed to security handling; non-security PR #88203 is kept related because its repair path depends on that quarantined issue.

actions.reason: Best classification is keep as a viable canonical PR candidate; merge cannot be recommended without a complete merge_preflight and fresh Codex /review.

Keep #88203 as a related non-security PR while exact issue #88111 is routed to security handling; the PR cannot be repaired or merged here because its approval-wait lineage depends on the quarantined issue and lacks merge preflight.
Exact security-sensitive hydrated ref must be routed to central OpenClaw security handling.
The remaining value of #92040 after #92053 and #93261 is a product/provider architecture decision with broad compatibility and auth-provider impact, not a narrow executable repair for this shard.
Keep as the canonical narrow Discord PR candidate. Merge is blocked until the executor obtains a complete merge_preflight and resolves the unstable merge state.

Requires maintainer product decision on whether to add the new Telegram spooled handler timeout config surface; current branch is dirty and proof failed, so it is not mergeable or repairable as a narrow automation task.

needs_human: #92040: decide whether any broader provider API-family thinking-policy work remains desirable after merged #92053 and #93261, given compatibility/auth-provider risk and maintainer rebase/drop-overlap request.
#87891: decide whether OpenClaw should publish the new Telegram spooled handler timeout config surface at all; proof and merge state are currently insufficient.
